name: From Pelayos de la Presa to the Embalse de Picadas via the Vía Verde de Alberche
name_original: Desde Pelayos de la Presa hasta el Embalse de Picadas por la Vía Verde de Alberche
region_intro: Hiking around San Martín De Valdeiglesias offers diverse landscapes in Madrid's Sierra Oeste, characterized by striking granitic rock formations and the presence of the Alberche and Cofio rivers. These rivers feed into significant reservoirs, including the Embalse de San Juan and Embalse de Picadas. The region features varied vegetation, from cork oak and pine forests in the mountainous north to Mediterranean scrubland in the central and southern parts. This area provides a blend of mountainous and fluvial environments for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around San Martín De Valdeiglesias The most popular hiking route is From Pelayos de la Presa to the Embalse de Picadas via the Vía Verde de Alberche, a 8.4 miles (13.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 34 minutes to complete. This moderate route utilizes an old railway line, offering scenic fluvial landscapes. Another top favourite among local hikers is The Route of the Coves loop from Pelayos de la Presa, an easy 3.6 miles (5.7 km) path. This route skirts small coves, providing relaxing views suitable for families. Local hikers also love the Río Alberche – Picadas Reservoir loop from San Juan, a 12.2 miles (19.6 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes and around the Picadas Reservoir, often completed in about 5 hours 23 minutes. Hiking around San Martín De Valdeiglesias is defined by granitic rock formations, the Alberche and Cofio rivers, and cork oak and pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy family walks to more challenging routes. The routes in San Martín De Valdeiglesias are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 800 hikers have used komoot to explore San Martín De Valdeiglesias's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in San Martín De Valdeiglesias?
A: San Martín De Valdeiglesias offers a diverse network of over 15 hiking trails. These routes cater to various fitness levels, including 3 easy trails, 11 moderate trails, and 4 more challenging options, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in San Martín De Valdeiglesias?
A: The region boasts a unique blend of mountainous and fluvial environments. You'll encounter striking granitic rock formations, the scenic Alberche and Cofio rivers, and significant reservoirs like the Embalse de San Juan and Embalse de Picadas. The vegetation varies from cork oak and pine forests in the north to Mediterranean scrubland in the central and southern parts.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for families in San Martín De Valdeiglesias?
A: Yes, there are several easy trails perfect for families. One popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21952367" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Route of the Coves loop from Pelayos de la Presa&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 5.7 km path skirting small coves and offering relaxing views. The Vía Verde de Alberche also provides gentle gradients suitable for all ages.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see along the hiking trails?
A: Hikers can explore a variety of natural and historical attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5313835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alberche Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, which follows an old railway line, offers scenic views. You might also encounter the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5662891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picadas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or the historic Castillo de la Coracera. For unique natural features, look out for the granitic rock formations characteristic of the area.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in San Martín De Valdeiglesias?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19434262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Martín de Valdeiglesias loop from San Martín de Valdeiglesias&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 13.4 km trail that offers a comprehensive experience of the local scenery.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in San Martín De Valdeiglesias?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, from riverine landscapes to pine forests, and the picturesque views of the reservoirs. The well-marked paths and variety of difficulty levels are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Is San Martín De Valdeiglesias a good place for birdwatching during a hike?
A: Absolutely. Much of the area is designated as a Special Protection Area for Birds (ZEPA) and a Site of Community Interest (LIC), part of the Natura 2000 network. This makes it an excellent spot for birdwatching, with species like the Iberian Imperial Eagle, Black Vulture, and Black Stork finding sanctuary here.

Q: Can I access hiking trails in San Martín De Valdeiglesias using public transport?
A: While specific public transport connections to every trailhead can vary, San Martín de Valdeiglesias itself is accessible by bus from Madrid. From the town, you may need to walk or arrange local transport to reach some of the more remote starting points. It's advisable to check local bus schedules in advance.

Q: Are there any opportunities for wild swimming after a hike?
A: Yes, the Embalse de San Juan (San Juan Reservoir) is particularly notable as one of the few places in the Community of Madrid where swimming is permitted, even boasting a Blue Flag beach. This makes it an ideal spot to cool off and relax after a hike.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in San Martín De Valdeiglesias?
A: The region offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, especially at midday, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended. Winter hiking is also possible, with cooler temperatures and a different kind of natural beauty.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, there are difficult routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31440145" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Alberche – Picadas Reservoir loop from San Juan&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 19.6 km trail that takes around 5 hours 23 minutes to complete, offering significant elevation changes and rewarding views.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in San Martín De Valdeiglesias?
A: Parking is generally available near the main trailheads and in the towns and villages that serve as starting points for hikes, such as San Martín de Valdeiglesias and Pelayos de la Presa. It's always a good idea to check specific route descriptions for detailed parking information.
tips: The Picadas reservoir is located between the municipalities of Pelayos de la Presa and Aldea del Fresno, about 60 km from Madrid, in the Sierra de Gredos. This reservoir collects the waters of the Alberche River and has a recreational area that the whole family will enjoy.
